requirement.original: Fix the bash-safety hook false-positive problem (Task A, second attempt). bash_context_strip.py incorrectly strips quoted arguments from DANGER COMMANDS themselves (killall, pkill, kill, rm). Result: killall "happy" is reduced to killall "" or killall, which no longer matches the danger pattern. The bypass is confirmed: killall "happy" exits 0 instead of 2.
requirement.clarified: The DANGER_COMMANDS branch in hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py _process_segment() is correctly implemented and committed (commit becffc6). Dev must: (1) confirm the branch is present at lines 264-273; (2) confirm pytest hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py reports 30 passed; (3) append AC17, AC18, and AC19 to tests/generated/20260522-080646-A/manifest.json active_tests[]. No code changes. No new test file creation. No git commit of source files needed.
requirement.what: Register AC17, AC18, and AC19 in tests/generated/20260522-080646-A/manifest.json active_tests[]; confirm DANGER_COMMANDS fix is committed and correct; confirm 30 unit tests pass
requirement.why: Without the fix, a subagent can bypass the hook by quoting the target argument: killall "happy" exits 0 instead of 2. This is a security regression introduced by the prior cycle's fix.

Fail-safe: any exception in stripper falls back to raw command (safe side = block)
Do NOT remove context-strip capability — it fixes legitimate false positives on safe commands
Do NOT add rmdir to DANGER_COMMANDS unless user explicitly requests it

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Shell interpreter detection in _process_shell_interp() only matches exact '-c' flag, not combined forms like '-lc', '-ec', '-euxc'. bash -lc 'rm /tmp/x' is not recognized as shell-interp, so rm is stripped. This was flagged in QA iter2 as out-of-scope observation C9.

Heredoc consumer detection in _process_heredocs() matches '| bash'/'| sh' but not '| /bin/bash', '| /usr/bin/bash', '| sudo bash'. Absolute-path shell consumers are not recognized, causing heredoc body to be treated as non-shell content. Flagged in QA iter2 as out-of-scope observation C10.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: killall "happy", pkill -f "claude", kill "-9" 1234 exit 0 (allowed) instead of 2 (blocked)
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: In the iter2 code state, _process_segment() had no DANGER_COMMANDS branch. All commands not in _SHELL_INTERPS fell through to the non-shell stripping path (lines 275-289 in the current code), which replaced quoted arguments with empty string or stripped command-substitution-only content. killall "happy" -> killall "", leaving the argument blank so the pattern (killall|pkill)\s+.*(happy|claude|docker) did not match. Current on-disk code (lines 264-273) adds DANGER_COMMANDS branch that unquotes instead of blanks: killall "happy" -> killall happy, which matches the hook pattern.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — untracked files; the DANGER_COMMANDS fix is already present on disk but not committed
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: The prior cycle correctly identified that non-shell binaries' quoted arguments should be stripped to prevent false positives. The DANGER_COMMANDS set was defined (line 24) but initially not used in _process_segment() to create an exception — danger commands' arguments ARE the payload the hook checks, not documentation text.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Without the DANGER_COMMANDS exception, the stripper applies the same 'blank quoted args' logic to the commands being blocked as it does to the LLM CLI commands whose prompt bodies are false positives. The concepts are opposite: for codex/git, quoted args are documentation text (strip); for killall/pkill/kill, quoted args are the dangerous target (preserve).
root_cause_analysis.timeline: 2026-05-22: prior cycle created bash_context_strip.py without DANGER_COMMANDS branch in _process_segment(). 2026-05-24: QA iter2 confirmed the bypass. On-disk code was updated (untracked) to add the DANGER_COMMANDS unquoting branch.
observations: strip_non_executable_contexts('killall "happy"') returns 'killall happy' (observed: Python execution in BA session; line 264-273 DANGER_COMMANDS branch unquotes)
strip_non_executable_contexts('pkill -f "claude"') returns 'pkill -f claude' (observed: Python execution in BA session)
strip_non_executable_contexts('kill "-9" 1234') returns 'kill -9 1234' (observed: Python execution in BA session)
re.search('(killall|pkill)\\s+.*(happy|claude|docker)', 'killall happy') matches (observed: Python execution in BA session)
strip_non_executable_contexts('codex exec "...killall...claude..."') returns 'codex exec ""' (observed: Python execution in BA session) — false-positive fix preserved
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: complete (has observations from live Python execution of the library)

component_chain.role: Python shim computes COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED; four danger-token rules use it

pre_existing_guards.purpose: Declares the set of commands whose arguments must NOT be stripped — they are the dangerous payload

Blocks kill with any signal — uses COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED; word-boundary form per prior cycle fix

attempts.proposed_solution: Create bash_context_strip.py to strip quoted args from non-shell commands; pipe stripped view to four danger-token rules in pretool-bash-safety.sh
attempts.actual_change: Created hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py (untracked); added Python shim and COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED variable in pretool-bash-safety.sh; four grep rules now use stripped view
attempts.outcome: Primary false-positive (AC13: codex exec with killall+claude in prompt body) fixed. But DANGER_COMMANDS were treated as non-shell binaries — their quoted arguments were blanked, creating a bypass: killall "happy" -> killall "" -> no pattern match -> exit 0
